name: Sonny Fortune
abstract: Cornelius "Sonny" Fortune (May 19, 1939 – October 25, 2018) was an American jazz saxophonist. He played soprano, alto, tenor, and baritone saxophones, clarinet, and flute.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet
Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet
JVC Jazz Festival: A Tribute to "Trane"

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: "In the band was – Sonny Fortune was in the band." and "Then you did one on Wolf, 1978, Innocence, with Sonny Fortune."
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: He had this gig at Pookie’s Pub ... with Elvin Jones and myself and Sonny Fortune -- working with Elvin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Fortune and I was playing in Italy one night... Just the two of us — saxophone and drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Tony Marino
about_quotes.evidence: And Sonny Fortune I remember. And the next day we were at breakfast and I’m sitting there and Reggie Workman comes in and I was thrilled to be sitting with him and Sonny Fortune.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tony Marino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 25, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Sonny Fortune. ... [INTERVIEWEE] I know. I heard about that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: And I think there’s stuff from Sonny Fortune there too, on Get Up with It.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
